sections.paragraph: m. with suff. קָדְקֳדֹו Job 2:7, and קָדְקְדֹו Psa 7:17 (where, however, other copies have קָדְקֳדֹו), the top of the head (so called from the hair being there divided and separated; compare Germ. Scheitel, die Haare fcheiteln; root קָדַד No. 1), Gen 49:26 Deu 33:16 fully קָדְקֹד שֵׂעָר the hairy crown of the head, Psa 68:22. Arab. مُقَدُّ part of the head, from the crown to the neck.
